title: bash-guard policy
description: Repo-specific parameters for the vendored bash-guard.sh. Everything here is per-repo; the guard core is identical across repos. Strict defaults apply when a key (or the whole file) is absent: an absent policy never weakens the guard.

agent_may_merge.description: Whether the agent may merge a PR whose base is the integration branch. A merge whose base is the protected branch is ALWAYS denied (human-only), regardless of this flag. false → the agent may not merge any PR (e.g. trunk→main repos, where the only base is the protected branch).

protected_branch.description: The reviewed/released line. Direct push is forbidden and merging a PR into it is human-only. Universal value 'main' in this fleet.

integration_branch.description: The live integration branch a merge may target when agent_may_merge is true (e.g. 'develop' for the products). Empty for trunk→main repos.

generated_trees.description: Paths of auto-generated trees that must not be hand-edited (e.g. a Prisma output dir). Empty → all generated-tree checks are skipped (short-circuit: an empty tree must never match every write).

generated_regen_hint.description: The command/instruction to regenerate the tree, shown in the deny message (e.g. 'edit schema.prisma and run pnpm build').

egress_allow.description: Hosts curl/wget may reach. Universal policy: network egress is restricted to this allow-list in every repo. Extend per-repo only with a ratified reason.

pr_score.description: Thresholds for the pr-score gate (scripts/gate.py). Absent → strict defaults (total_min 22, axis_min 4). Merge permission is NOT here — it is agent_may_merge above, which the gate also honors (false → a passing score still escalates to the human).

total_min.description: Minimum sum across the 5 axes (each 1-5, max 25) for an auto-merge verdict.

axis_min.description: Minimum score every single axis must reach for an auto-merge verdict.
